operating_state: NIKE achieved record revenues of $46.7 billion in fiscal 2022, increasing 5% reported and 6% currency-neutral, driven by NIKE Direct growth of 14% to represent 42% of total revenues with digital sales reaching $10.7 billion. Regional performance was broadly positive with North America up, EMEA increasing with physical retail recovery, and APLA rising, while Greater China declined due to COVID-19 restrictions. Wholesale revenues declined as the company navigated supply chain constraints and inventory management challenges.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 120 basis points to 46.0% driven by NIKE Direct margin expansion, higher full-price sales mix and favorable foreign currency impacts, partially offset by elevated freight and logistics costs and higher inventory obsolescence reserves in Greater China. Selling and administrative expenses increased due to higher strategic technology investments, wage-related expenses, NIKE Direct variable costs and normalized demand creation spending. Manufacturing and transportation costs create margin sensitivity alongside commodity price volatility.
material_events: NIKE completed $4.0 billion in share repurchases under the four-year program and received board authorization for new four-year $18 billion share repurchase program. The company entered definitive agreements to sell legal entities in Argentina, Uruguay and Chile to third-party distributors with closures expected by Q3 fiscal 2023, and deconsolidated Russian legal entities due to economic sanctions resulting in $96 million pre-tax charge before deciding to exit the Russian marketplace.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 factory shutdowns in Vietnam and Indonesia during Q1 eliminated approximately three months of production, driving inventory to $8.4 billion up 23% with extended transit times elevating in-transit inventory levels. Foreign currency translation provided $132 million favorable impact on pre-tax income through international profit translation and hedging activities, while geopolitical conflicts create additional currency volatility. Central bank interest rate increases may impact borrowing costs, while global economic deterioration could reduce discretionary consumer spending.
forward_outlook: Management expects inventory transit times and logistics costs to remain elevated with product costs staying high due to increased input costs, while Q1 fiscal 2023 gross margin could face negative impact from increased promotional activity. The company maintains multi-year investment plans for digital transformation and brand campaigns while continuing Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy execution. Annual capital expenditures expected at approximately 3% of revenues focused on digital capabilities and supply chain improvements.
uncertainty_profile: Operating environment volatility expected to continue with ongoing COVID-19 variant risks potentially disrupting operations and supply chain constraints requiring continued mitigation. Climate change regulations may necessitate additional facility investments while cybersecurity risks have increased due to remote work arrangements. Outstanding endorsement and product purchase obligations represent significant future cash commitments, while retail market consolidation concentrates credit risk among fewer large retailers.
